mechanism_narrative: Persistent increases in the Consumer Price Index signal rising inflation expectations, prompting bond market participants to demand higher yields on 10-year Treasury notes to compensate for eroded purchasing power over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL level and subsequent 20-day yield change is below 0.10 over any rolling 3-year window, or the Rank IC of this signal is less than 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold indicates economic distress and triggers a flight-to-safety into Treasury bonds, compressing yields downward over the following 20 trading days as investors seek safe assets.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, the 20-day yield change is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average yield change is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts and industrial production reflects broad economic momentum from both residential investment and manufacturing output, raising capital demand and pushing 10-year yields higher via increased growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and INDPRO report month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5%
failure_condition: When both HOUST and INDPRO grow >0.5% MoM, the 60-day yield change is negative in >50% of cases, or the Rank IC of the combined signal is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment where the fed funds rate is near zero, any increase in the policy rate signals the start of monetary normalization, raising term premiums and causing 10-year yields to rise over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points month-over-month
failure_condition: In the zero lower bound regime, instances with FEDFUNDS increase >25bp lead to negative 20-day yield changes in >50% of cases, or the average effect is not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payroll gains above a threshold indicate robust labor market conditions, raising inflation expectations and the likelihood of future Fed tightening, thereby pushing 10-year yields higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS change >200k, the 5-day yield change is negative in >50% of such events, or the signal has no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales and industrial production indicates synchronized expansion in consumption and manufacturing, reinforcing growth expectations and raising the term premium on 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and INDPRO show MoM growth >0.3%
failure_condition: When both signals are positive, 20-day yield changes are not on average positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the combined Z-score strategy yields Sharpe < 0.5 over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production expands aggregate supply capacity, alleviating supply constraints and reducing inflation expectations, which leads to lower 10-year yields over the next quarter as the bond market prices in less inflation risk.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year window Spearman correlation between INDPRO growth and subsequent 60-day yield change is not significantly negative (p < 0.05), or the Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the fed funds rate is already elevated above 4%, further rate increases risk slowing the economy and reducing inflation expectations, causing 10-year yields to decline as markets anticipate a policy-induced economic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 25bp when current level is above 4%
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS >4% and increases 25bp, 20-day yield changes are positive in >50% of cases, or the average yield change is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
